messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: 🔴 [SEV1] checkout-service: p99 latency exceeded 5000 ms SLO threshold. Runbook: https://wiki/checkout-latency

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Taking this. Checking dashboards now.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Seeing massive 429 spike on the ingress. This looks like the WAF or a DDoS. @frank_nguyen can you check CloudFlare?

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: CloudFlare is clean. No traffic anomaly externally. Traffic profile looks normal.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Interesting. So the 429s are being generated internally. Checking rate limiter metrics.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: Wait, there was a PR merged this morning — rate limiter config change. PR #112. Changing from sliding window to fixed window.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Yeah I see it. Fixed window counter is global not per-user. As soon as we hit 100 req total in a minute window, EVERYONE gets blocked. Classic.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: So we're throttling ourselves. The limit was meant to be per-user but it's actually shared. Yikes.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Rolling back rate_limiter.py to previous commit. Deploying now.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: 429s dropping. Latency recovering. Looks like the rollback is working.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Confirmed. Error rate back to baseline. Incident resolved. Will write postmortem.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: One thing I noticed — the test only covered a single-user scenario. We need a load test that validates multi-user behavior for rate limiter changes.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Good catch. Adding that to the postmortem action items.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: 🔴 [SEV1] order-service: error rate exceeded 50% for 5 minutes. Current: 88%.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: On it. order-service is throwing 500s. DB connection errors in logs: 'QueuePool limit overflow'. Checking DB health.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: RDS metrics look fine — CPU at 22%, no long queries. This isn't a DB performance issue.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Connection pool is exhausted. Max 20 connections, all in use. Why are we using 20 connections simultaneously?

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: There was a deploy 15 minutes ago. The inventory availability check PR. Let me look at it... oh no.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Found it. For every item in a cart, we're opening a new DB connection to inventory-service. Cart with 10 items = 10 concurrent connections. At 300 checkouts/min that's 3000 simultaneous connections.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: And we have a connection pool of 20. We blow past that in like 2 orders. Plus connections aren't being closed on error paths.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: I'm looking at inventory_client.py. Yep. No finally block. Connection leaked on every exception.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Two fixes needed: 1) add finally: conn.close(), 2) batch the inventory lookups into a single query. Deploying fix 1 first as a hotfix.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Fix 1 deployed. Error rate dropping. Down to 35%.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Fix 2 deployed (batch query). Error rate at 1.2%. Back to normal. Phew.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: We should add a connection pool utilization alert. This would have fired 5 minutes before the incident escalated.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Agreed. Adding to postmortem. Also need load tests that simulate multi-item carts.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: ⚠️ [SEV2] auth-service: pod auth-service-7d9f8b-xkp2q OOMKilled. Third OOM in 18 hours.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Another OOM. Memory grows linearly then crashes. Not a traffic spike — traffic is flat. Something is accumulating.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: Could be a connection leak? Auth-service makes a lot of outbound connections.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Checked connection count. Stable. It's heap memory growing. I'm pulling a heap dump from the next pod before it OOMs.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: Got the heap dump. Analyzing with MAT... LRUCache is the biggest object. 50,000 entries. Each ~42 KB. That's 2.1 GB right there.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: The JWT cache PR! Was merged 3 days ago. Been gradually filling up ever since. The PR said 50 MB estimate but it's clearly wrong.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: Yeah the estimate assumed 1 KB per entry but a full JWT payload with roles is way larger. And there's no TTL so entries never leave.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: With 50,000 daily active users each getting a new token, the cache fills up and then just sits there forever.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Immediate fix: add 5-minute TTL and cap memory at 150 MB. Deploying now.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Deployed. Memory stabilized at 315 MB. OOM-kill loop stopped.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: We need better tooling for cache sizing estimates. The PR calculation was way off.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: And a memory growth alert. If a pod grows by more than X MB/hour, we should know before it OOMs.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: 🔴 [SEV1] notification-service: thread pool at 100%. CPU sustained at 98% for 10 minutes.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: notification-service is thrashing. Checking what's filling the thread pool.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: All threads are in retry backoff loops. SendGrid is returning 503.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Is SendGrid having an outage? Checking their status page.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: SendGrid status shows 'Investigating elevated error rates' as of 35 minutes ago. But why are we still hammering them?

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: We should have backed off by now. Wait — was the circuit breaker removed last week?

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: ...yes it was. PR #287. 'remove Hystrix circuit breaker'. Without it, we just retry infinitely.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: And the retry backoff is per-thread in memory. So 2000 threads all in different phases of backoff = constant hammering. We're actually making SendGrid worse.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: We need to stop the retries immediately. I'm deploying an emergency circuit breaker via env vars — CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED=true CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD=10.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: Thread pool clearing. CPU dropping. SendGrid calls stopped.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: SendGrid recovered. We can start replaying the dead-letter queue, but slowly — 100 msg/min max.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Replay started. Email backlog clearing. Incident contained.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: Lessons learned: removing resilience patterns needs more scrutiny. The Hystrix removal PR was merged without SRE review.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Adding SRE mandatory review to any PR touching resilience patterns. And circuit breaker as a required checklist item for external clients.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: ⚠️ [SEV2] checkout-service: revenue anomaly detected — 14% below forecast for past 4 hours.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Revenue alert. Checking checkout error rates... looks normal. No 5xx spike. Customers are completing checkouts but... at wrong prices?

messages.author: kate_brennan
messages.text: Support just escalated — 47 tickets about flash sale discount not showing. The sale started at 09:00. It's 13:00 now.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Product page shows the discounted price. Cart shows full price. Smells like a caching issue.

messages.author: liam_foster
messages.text: Checked the price cache — it's serving prices from last night. Cache TTL was recently changed to 24h. But the webhook should bust the cache on price updates.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Let me check webhook delivery logs... oh. The webhook has been returning 401 for 6 days.

messages.author: kate_brennan
messages.text: The service account token was rotated in the security audit last week. Webhook was never updated. Silent failure — no alerting on the 401s.

messages.author: liam_foster
messages.text: So for 6 days all price updates have been going nowhere. The 24h TTL means the cache is serving week-old prices.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Two actions: 1) rotate webhook token right now, 2) flush the price cache entirely.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Token updated. Running cache flush... done. Prices are now current.

messages.author: kate_brennan
messages.text: Revenue recovering. Checkout prices now match product pages. Flash sale discount visible.

messages.author: liam_foster
messages.text: We need monitoring on that webhook. If it starts returning 4xx we need to know immediately, not 6 days later.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Also, the TTL change from 5min to 24h made the blast radius of a broken webhook enormous. We should revert TTL to 5min until we have proper invalidation health monitoring.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: ⚠️ [SEV2] order-service: DB CPU at 99%. Slow query log flooded.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: DB is on fire. Checking slow query log... thousands of 'SELECT * FROM products WHERE id = ?' queries. All from order-service.

messages.author: henry_silva
messages.text: Was there a recent deploy? Checking deployment history... order-service deployed 14 minutes ago. 'order history enrichment' feature.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Let me look at order_history.py... ProductService.get_by_id() called in a loop for each item in each order. Classic N+1.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: A user with 20 orders × 6 items per order = 120 SELECTs just to load their order history page. And we have 700 concurrent users.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: 700 × 120 = 84,000 queries/minute. Our DB handles 6,000. We're 14× over capacity.

messages.author: henry_silva
messages.text: Can we feature-flag this off while we fix it?

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: No feature flag in place. I'm reverting the deploy. ETA 3 minutes.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: Revert deployed. DB CPU dropping. 45%... 28%... back to 17%. Read replica catching up.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Good. Now let's fix the query properly. We need a single batch query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id IN (all_item_ids_in_all_orders).

messages.author: henry_silva
messages.text: Fix implemented and tested. Reducing 120 queries to 1 per page load. Deploying with query count assertion in the test.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Fix deployed. Order history p99 back to 130ms. DB CPU at 16%. All good.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: We need to add query count assertions to any endpoint touching ORM loops. This is the third N+1 this quarter.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: 🔴 [SEV1] auth-service: availability 0% for 8 minutes. All health checks failing.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: auth-service completely down. Checking pods... pods are running but not serving traffic. gRPC health check failing.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Logs: 'tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate has expired'. TLS certificate expired??

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: Certificate expired? cert-manager should handle this. Let me check cert-manager logs.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: cert-manager logs: 'Not renewing certificate auth-service-tls, not within renewBefore window'. The window is 7 days. Cert expires today.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: But renewBefore is 7 days... the cert should have been renewed 7 days ago. Unless the cert had more than 7 days left when the config changed?

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Found it. PR #341 changed renewBefore from 30d to 7d. At the time of the PR the cert had 23 days left. Outside the new 7-day window. cert-manager never triggered renewal. Then the cert just expired naturally.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: So the config change created a dead zone between 7 and 30 days. Any cert in that range gets stranded.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: I'm manually forcing renewal now: kubectl annotate cert... done. cert-manager is issuing new cert.

messages.author: carol_kim
messages.text: New cert propagated. auth-service responding. Login success rate 100%.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: We need a cert expiry alert at 14 days, not just 7. And a CI check that validates the renewBefore policy won't strand existing certs.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: Also, any cert-manager config change should trigger a full audit of all managed certs to check for stranding.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: 🔴 [SEV1] payment-service: webhook endpoint returning HTTP 400 for 100% of Stripe events.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Stripe webhooks all failing with 400. Checking payment-service logs.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: Logs: 'SignatureVerificationError: No signatures found matching the expected signature for payload'. Webhook signature validation is broken.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Did Stripe change their signing format? Checking Stripe changelog... no changes on their side.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: payment-service had a deploy this afternoon. Stripe SDK v3 upgrade. Let me check the v3 migration guide.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Found it. v3 changelog says: 'construct_event() now requires raw bytes, not parsed string'. We're passing request.json() which re-serializes the body.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Re-serialization changes whitespace and key ordering. HMAC over different bytes = signature mismatch. Every time.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: And the canary didn't catch this because no webhooks were delivered to the canary instance during the 30-min soak window.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Fix: change request.json() to request.get_data(as_text=False). Deploying hotfix.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Stripe webhooks now returning 200. Webhook delivery recovering.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Now we need to replay missed events. Pulling event IDs from Stripe API for the last 6 hours.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: 847 events replayed. Order statuses reconciled. Revenue impact under assessment.

messages.author: alice_chen
messages.text: Lesson: mock-based webhook tests don't catch raw-vs-parsed body issues. We need integration tests with actual raw bytes from Stripe fixtures.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: ⚠️ [SEV2] gateway-api: p99 latency 30,000 ms across all endpoints. SLO breach.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Everything is timing out at exactly 30 seconds. Not random — exactly 30s. That's a configured timeout value.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: Was there a gateway deploy today? Checking... yes, NGINX config change for the ML recommendations endpoint. 30-second timeout.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Checking the nginx.conf diff... the proxy_read_timeout 30s is in the http block, not in the location block. It's global.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: So all endpoints now have a 30-second timeout. Requests that should fail at 3s are now hanging for 30s. Thread pool fills up.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Rolling back the gateway configmap now. kubectl rollout undo deployment/gateway-api.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Rollback complete. Latency recovering. 8s... 4s... back to normal.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: I'll reapply the config correctly — timeout inside the location block only.

messages.author: eve_thompson
messages.text: That's live. /api/v2/recommendations gets 30s, everything else stays at 3s.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: All clear. Gateway healthy.

messages.author: iris_nakamura
messages.text: We need mandatory nginx-config-review for gateway changes. And a staging soak that actually exercises all endpoint types, not just the changed one.

messages.author: dave_patel
messages.text: Adding those to the gateway PR template as required steps.

messages.author: pagerduty-bot
messages.text: 🔴 [SEV1] payment-service: error rate 23%. DB deadlock errors spiking.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Payment failures at 23%. MySQL deadlock errors. Multiple transactions are locking each other.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: Deadlock graph from information_schema shows: T1 holds idempotency_keys row lock, waiting on payment_transactions. T2 holds payment_transactions lock, waiting on idempotency_keys. Classic AB/BA.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: The idempotency PR was merged yesterday. That introduced the idempotency_keys table with SELECT FOR UPDATE.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: The lock is held for the duration of the Stripe API call. Stripe takes 2-8 seconds. So you have a lock held for up to 8 seconds. If a retry comes in during that window — deadlock.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: And mobile clients retry aggressively. Each deadlock triggers 3 retries, which create more deadlocks. It's a feedback loop.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: I'm adding innodb_lock_wait_timeout=2 immediately. That'll at least fail fast instead of holding for 8 seconds.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: Error rate still 18%. Failing faster but still failing.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: We need to fix the root cause. The Stripe call cannot happen inside a transaction holding a row lock. Replace SELECT FOR UPDATE with INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE.

messages.author: grace_okonkwo
messages.text: Fix deployed. Optimistic lock replaces pessimistic. Stripe call is now outside the transaction.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Error rate dropping. 8%... 3%... 0.1%. Back to baseline.

messages.author: frank_nguyen
messages.text: Also pushed mobile SDK update with retry jitter (100-500ms random backoff). That'll reduce the thundering herd if we ever deadlock again.

messages.author: bob_martinez
messages.text: Confirmed stable. Writing incident report now.
